rag_rules: Place input range assumptions in component type annexes using visible feature names and concrete bounds.
Use assign statements only for implementation-level output bindings with existing features or declared variables.
Generate syntactically valid AGREE contracts within annex agree blocks using valid AADL scope identifiers.
Avoid placing guarantee statements in implementation scope; use assumptions and equations instead.
generic_rules: Ensure all AGREE expressions reference visible identifiers from the AADL model.
Validate that assumption and guarantee types match the actual data types of referenced features.
Use named constants or visible relations when defining range constraints in AGREE contracts.
Do not include AADL property names as AGREE variables unless explicitly exposed in the model.
syntax_or_pattern_reminders: AGREE contracts must be enclosed in `annex agree {** ... **};` blocks.
Assumptions and guarantees should reference valid component ports or declared variables.
Boolean expressions in AGREE must match the data types of the referenced features.
Implementation scope contracts should only include assign statements for output features.
anti_patterns: Do not place output range guarantees in component type contracts unless they are derived from input assumptions.
Avoid using AADL property names like Period or Deadline as AGREE variables.
Do not generate assumptions or guarantees that reference opaque Boolean or enumeration data without numeric scalar representation.
Do not mix implementation-level bindings with type-level assumptions in the same annex block.
